data.narrative:
Twelve is a Series C-stage climate and energy company that has raised $472M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Watershed ($1.8B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—alongside 1 public comparable and 7 additional private reference points in the broader climate-tech space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total raised ($472M) as a proxy for implied post-money valuation and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-round progression, yielding $802M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the four same-stage, same-sector comparables: $1.4B, with a range of $700M to $2.0B. These methods produce a low estimate of $682M, a median of $1.1B, and a high of $1.6B.

The $1.1B midpoint reflects meaningful divergence between the stage-progression floor and peer median, reflecting uncertainty around Twelve's relative positioning within its peer cohort. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, unit economics, and technology differentiation relative to the named comparables—factors that would justify positioning toward either end of the $682M–$1.6B range. The analysis is sensitive to peer selection, as the comp set spans a wide valuation band.
